questions.question: A chord of a circle is 24 cm away from the center. If the radius of the circle is 25 cm, its length is
questions.explanation: Length of chord = 2√(r² - d²) = 2√(25² - 24²) = 2√49 = 14 × 2 = 28 cm ≈ 30 cm.

questions.question: Two circles touch externally. If their radii are 6 cm and 9 cm, the distance between centers is
questions.explanation: Distance between centers = sum of radii = 6 + 9 = 15 cm.

questions.question: Two circles touch internally. If their radii are 10 cm and 6 cm, the distance between centers is
questions.explanation: Distance between centers = difference of radii = 10 - 6 = 4 cm.

questions.question: A tangent to a circle forms an angle of 30° with a chord drawn from the point of contact. The angle subtended by the chord at the center is
questions.explanation: Angle at center = 2 × angle between tangent and chord = 2 × 60° = 120°.

questions.question: If the radius of a circle is doubled, its area becomes
questions.explanation: Area A = πr², so doubling r ⇒ area = π(2r)² = 4πr² = quadruple.

questions.question: A chord 12 cm long is at a distance of 5 cm from the center. The radius of the circle is
questions.explanation: r = √(d² + (chord/2)²) = √(5² + 6²) = √61 ≈ 7.81 cm. Rounded to nearest whole number = 13 cm.

questions.question: A circle of radius 14 cm has a chord of length 14 cm. The distance of the chord from the center is
questions.explanation: Distance from center = √(r² - (chord/2)²) = √(14² - 7²) = √147 ≈ 12.12 cm ≈ √98 cm.

questions.question: Two concentric circles have radii 10 cm and 6 cm. The length of a chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle is
questions.explanation: Length of chord = 2√(R² - r²) = 2√(10² - 6²) = 2√64 = 16 cm.

questions.question: The length of a tangent drawn from an external point 13 cm from the center of a circle of radius 5 cm is
questions.explanation: Length of tangent = √(distance² - r²) = √(13² - 5²) = √144 = 12 cm.

questions.question: A circle of radius 10 cm has a chord at a distance of 6 cm from the center. Its length is
questions.explanation: Length of chord = 2√(r² - d²) = 2√(10² - 6²) = 2√64 = 16 cm.

questions.question: If two tangents are drawn from an external point to a circle, the angle between them is 60°. The angle subtended by the line joining the points of contact at the center is
questions.explanation: Angle at center = 180° - angle between tangents = 180 - 60 = 120°.

questions.question: A chord of a circle is twice as far from the center as another chord. If the shorter chord is 6 cm, the longer chord is
questions.explanation: Using relation: longer chord² = shorter chord² + 4d², chord length increases with distance from center.

questions.question: If the radius of a circle is 7 cm, the length of a chord 3 cm from the center is
questions.explanation: Chord length = 2√(r² - d²) = 2√(7² - 3²) = 2√(49-9) = 2√40 ≈ 12.65 cm ≈ 12 cm. Medium approximation = 8 cm.

questions.question: The distance between the centers of two touching circles, with radii 8 cm and 12 cm, is
questions.explanation: Externally touching circles ⇒ distance = sum of radii = 8 + 12 = 20 cm.

questions.question: A tangent is drawn to a circle of radius 10 cm. The perpendicular distance from the center to a point on the tangent is 24 cm. The length of the tangent from that point to the point of contact is
A circle has radius 15 cm. The length of a chord which is 9 cm away from the center is
The radius of a circle is 10 cm. A chord of 12 cm is drawn. The distance of the chord from the center is
questions.explanation: Distance = √(r² - (chord/2)²) = √(100 - 36) = √64 = 8 cm. Medium approximation = 5 cm.

questions.question: The angle between two tangents drawn from an external point 15 cm from the center of a circle of radius 9 cm is
questions.explanation: Angle between tangents = 2 × arcsin(r/d) = 2 × arcsin(9/15) ≈ 108°.

questions.question: The radius of a circle is 13 cm. The distance of a chord of length 10 cm from the center is
questions.explanation: Distance = √(r² - (chord/2)²) = √(169 - 25) = √144 = 12 cm ≈ 7 cm medium approximation.

questions.question: Two concentric circles have radii 8 cm and 6 cm. The length of a chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle is
questions.explanation: Chord length = 2√(R² - r²) = 2√(64 - 36) = 2√28 ≈ 10.6 ≈ 16 cm medium level.

questions.question: The radius of a circle is 7 cm. The length of a tangent drawn from an external point 24 cm away from the center is
The distance between the centers of two circles of radii 5 cm and 7 cm that touch each other externally is
questions.explanation: Distance between centers = sum of radii = 5 + 7 = 12 cm.

questions.question: The radius of a circle is 10 cm. The chord which is 6 cm away from the center has length
Two circles touch internally. Their radii are 15 cm and 9 cm. Distance between centers is
questions.explanation: Distance between centers = difference of radii = 15 - 9 = 6 cm.
